title: Uragoga nicaraguensis
intro: Uragoga nicaraguensis, also known as Guayacán or Nicaragua's tree, is a plant species native to Central America, belonging to the family Passifloraceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Uragoga nicaraguensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Uragoga nicaraguensis is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or decoction, which are used to treat fever, colds, and other respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Uragoga nicaraguensis is classified within the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This plant belongs to the genus Uragoga, which is a part of the Apocynaceae family. The classification of Uragoga nicaraguensis is further divided into the order Gentianales, class Magnoliopsida, and division Magnoliophyta. The species Uragoga nicaraguensis is native to Nicaragua and is known for its unique characteristics. Uragoga nicaraguensis is also known by its synonym, Aspidosperma nicaraguense.
